title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
format_notes: Baseline extracted from revised final shooting script; for Awesome-O agent pipelines.
logline: A reprogrammed Terminator must protect young John Connor from a shape-shifting liquid-metal assassin sent from the future—while John's mother, Sarah, wages her own war to stop the invention that creates Skynet.
themes: Fate versus choice; rewriting the future
What makes someone human (empathy, learning, sacrifice)
Maternal ferocity and trauma after survival
Technology as neutral—intent and stewardship matter
central_conflict: Prevent John Connor's assassination in the present while deciding whether to destroy the technology that will cause Judgment Day—and whether a machine can be trusted as family.

primary_places: Pescadero State Hospital for the Criminally Insane

protagonists.name: Sarah Connor
protagonists.role: Mother of John; traumatized warrior preparing him for war
protagonists.want: Stop Judgment Day and protect John; control what she cannot predict

protagonists.name: John Connor
protagonists.role: Pre-teen future leader; moral compass for the group
protagonists.want: Survive; refuse to become only a weapon; teach the machine humanity

key_antagonist.name: T-1000 (liquid metal terminator)
key_antagonist.role: Skynet assassin impersonating humans; relentless pursuit

key_ally.name: Terminator (Series 800, reprogrammed)
key_ally.role: Protector; starts as tool, becomes surrogate father figure through John's teaching

stakes: If John dies, humanity loses the Resistance leader; if Skynet's path stays unchanged, nuclear war and machine rule follow.
